entries.title: Placement Support Overview
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How does placement support work?
What is placement support?
entries.content: Placement readiness combines resume quality, communication practice, role-specific preparation, and application tracking. Students improve faster when they focus on one target role at a time and review feedback after each interview stage.

entries.title: Resume Audit Basics
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: What happens in a resume audit?
How should I review my resume?
entries.content: A resume audit checks clarity, grammar, relevance, measurable achievements, and consistency with the target role. Students should replace vague lines with impact statements that show scale, tools used, and outcomes delivered.

entries.title: Resume Bullet Improvement
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I improve resume bullets?
What makes a strong resume bullet?
entries.content: Strong resume bullets begin with action, mention the task context, and end with measurable impact wherever possible. A bullet such as 'built API used by 2,000 users' is usually more useful than 'worked on backend development.'

entries.title: Portfolio Project Selection
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: Which projects should I show in my portfolio?
How do I choose portfolio projects?
entries.content: A good portfolio shows two or three projects that match the target role and demonstrate ownership, problem solving, and measurable results. Students should prefer complete projects they can explain deeply over a long list of unfinished experiments.

entries.title: LinkedIn Profile Cleanup
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I improve my LinkedIn profile?
What should a good student LinkedIn profile include?
entries.content: A clean LinkedIn profile should have a professional photo, clear headline, short summary, updated projects, and consistent dates. Students should also add links to GitHub, portfolio work, or certifications if those strengthen the target profile.

entries.title: Application Tracker Usage
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: Why should I keep an application tracker?
What should I track in my job applications?
entries.content: An application tracker should record company name, role, date applied, interview stage, recruiter details, deadlines, and feedback. This prevents missed follow-ups and helps students notice patterns in the roles where they are getting shortlisted.

entries.title: Aptitude Practice Routine
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I practice aptitude for placements?
What is a good aptitude routine?
entries.content: Aptitude preparation works best with timed practice in quant, reasoning, and verbal sections plus weekly error review. Students should learn shortcuts only after understanding the logic behind common problem types.

entries.title: Coding Round Preparation
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I prepare for coding rounds?
What should I focus on for placement coding tests?
entries.content: Coding round preparation should cover arrays, strings, recursion, hash maps, sorting, and basic dynamic programming at an appropriate level. Students should practice writing clean code under time limits instead of only reading solved answers.

entries.title: Technical Interview Structure
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: What happens in a technical interview?
How is a technical interview usually structured?
entries.content: A technical interview often includes project discussion, concept checks, problem solving, and follow-up questions on tradeoffs. Students should prepare to explain why they made decisions, not just what technology they used.

entries.title: Mock Interview Value
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: Why are mock interviews useful?
How often should I do mock interviews?
entries.content: Mock interviews help students improve answer clarity, confidence, body language, and time control under pressure. Feedback is most useful when it identifies specific weak areas such as rambling, shallow project explanations, or lack of examples.

entries.title: Behavioral Interview STAR Method
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: What is the STAR method?
How do I answer behavioral questions?
entries.content: The STAR method structures answers around Situation, Task, Action, and Result so responses stay clear and complete. Students should prepare a few real stories covering teamwork, conflict, leadership, failure, and learning.

entries.title: HR Interview Preparation
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I prepare for HR interviews?
What matters in an HR round?
entries.content: HR interviews test motivation, communication, role understanding, and professionalism more than technical depth. Students should answer honestly, keep examples concrete, and avoid giving inconsistent reasons for career choices.

entries.title: Group Discussion Preparation
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I prepare for group discussions?
What makes a good GD performance?
entries.content: Good group discussion performance combines listening, concise speaking, structure, and respectful participation. Students do not need to dominate; they need to add value, support points with logic, and help the discussion stay focused.

entries.title: Company Research Before Interview
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: Why should I research a company before interview?
What should I know about a company before interviewing?
entries.content: Before an interview, students should know the company products, role expectations, recent work areas, and basic hiring process. This helps them tailor project examples and ask stronger questions at the end of the interview.

entries.title: Internship to PPO Strategy
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How can an internship lead to a PPO?
What should I do during an internship to improve PPO chances?
entries.content: Students improve PPO chances by taking ownership, documenting progress, asking useful questions, and delivering visible outcomes during the internship. Regular communication with mentors and clean handover of work also make a strong impression.

entries.title: Resume Summary Drafting
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I write a resume summary?
What should a student profile summary include?
entries.content: A resume summary should be two or three lines that describe your target role, core skills, and one proof of impact. It works best when it is specific, such as 'backend developer focused on APIs, databases, and test automation' rather than generic praise.

entries.title: GitHub Portfolio Cleanup
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I clean up my GitHub portfolio?
What makes a recruiter-friendly GitHub profile?
entries.content: A clean GitHub portfolio highlights a few complete repositories with readable READMEs, clear commit history, and simple setup instructions. Students should pin their best work and remove placeholder projects that do not help the recruiter understand the target role.

entries.title: System Design Basics for Students
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: What are system design basics for interviews?
How should a student start learning system design?
entries.content: System design basics for students focus on requirements, scale, storage, caching, APIs, and failure handling. For interview prep, it is better to explain one or two tradeoffs clearly than to memorize a large list of buzzwords.

entries.title: Coding Interview Debugging
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I debug during a coding interview?
What if my coding solution has a bug?
entries.content: When a coding interview solution fails, students should first explain the intended approach, then isolate the bug, and finally correct the edge case. Interviewers usually value calm reasoning and clean fixes more than rushing into a new answer.

entries.title: Salary Negotiation Basics
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I negotiate salary?
What should I know before discussing an offer?
entries.content: Salary negotiation starts with understanding your market range, the role level, and the complete offer package including bonuses and benefits. Students should be polite, ask for time to review, and negotiate with facts rather than pressure.

entries.title: Rejection Follow-up Etiquette
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: Should I follow up after rejection?
How do I respond politely after a rejection?
entries.content: A good follow-up after rejection should thank the recruiter, ask for one improvement point, and keep the tone professional. This keeps the relationship open for future roles and shows maturity even when the result was disappointing.

entries.title: Referral Request Strategy
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I ask for a referral?
What should I include in a referral request?
entries.content: A referral request should be short, specific, and respectful. Students should mention the role, explain why they fit, and attach a concise resume or portfolio so the request is easy to act on.

entries.title: Aptitude Formula Revision
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I revise aptitude formulas?
What formula topics matter for placement tests?
entries.content: Aptitude revision works best when formulas are grouped by topic such as ratio, percentage, time and work, probability, and geometry. Students should practice recall from memory before looking at notes so the formulas stay usable during timed tests.

entries.title: Internship Feedback to PPO
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How do I improve PPO chances during internship?
Why should I ask for feedback in an internship?
entries.content: To improve PPO chances during internship, students should ask for weekly feedback, fix issues quickly, and document the value they delivered. A visible habit of ownership often matters more than simply being present every day.

entries.title: Group Project Storytelling
entries.author: Nupur Team
sample_queries: How should I explain a group project in interviews?
What makes a good project story?
entries.content: Group project storytelling means explaining the problem, your role, the team's decisions, and the final result in a clear sequence. Recruiters understand teamwork better when the story shows ownership, conflict resolution, and measurable outcome.
